articles.title: March of the Harmattan
articles.summary: Strong winds in March 2026 carried Saharan dust across northwestern Africa and toward the Canary Islands, reducing visibility and prompting alerts.

articles.title: Live coverage: NASA to launch Artemis 2, its first Moon-bound mission with astronauts since 1972
articles.summary: The four astronauts will spend more than nine days onboard their Orion spacecraft and is poised to pass the distance record set by Apollo 13 in 1970. Liftoff from Launch Complex 39B is scheduled for Wednesday, April 1, at 6:24 p.m. EDT (2224 UTC).

articles.title: Virgin Galactic expects commercial suborbital flights to resume late this year
articles.summary:
Virgin Galactic still expects to resume commercial suborbital flights by the end of the year as its first next-generation spaceplane nears completion.
The post Virgin Galactic expects commercial suborbital flights to resume late this year appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Cast your vote for the best sci-fi spaceship and then watch debaters duke it out at Space Symposium
articles.summary:
Voting ends April 14. Resistance is futile – you must comply.

articles.title: Christine Klein takes up duty as acting Director of Controlling, Finance and Operational Procurement
articles.summary: Christine Klein took up duty as acting Director of Controlling, Finance and Operational Procurement (D/CFO) at the European Space Agency on 1 April 2026. She will lead the newly established directorate during its consolidation.

articles.title: Everything Still on Track for Artemis II Launch to the Moon on April 1
articles.summary: Just over a day from now, four astronauts may well be on their way to the Moon. Weather continues to be 80 percent favorable for launch at 6:24 pm ET […]

articles.title: This is my third Orion launch, but it feels totally different
articles.summary: The first two launches of Orion felt hollow, but NASA is finally on a better course.

articles.title: Watch live: Artemis II launch
articles.summary: The first launch opportunity for Artemis II, the first mission to bring astronauts towards the Moon in over 50 years, is set for 1 April at 18:24 local time (2 April at 00:24 CEST). Tune in from one hour before launch at 22:24 BST / 23:24 CEST on ESA Web TV to watch the launch.
